name: Bufones de Pría Route
name_original: Paseo por los Bufones de Pría — circular por la Playa de San Antonio
description: Hike the moderate Bufones de Pría Route, a 9.3-mile coastal trail in Asturias featuring dramatic blowholes and beautiful beaches.
region_intro: Hiking around Llanes offers diverse terrain, from a rugged coastline with dramatic cliffs and sandy beaches to the foothills of the Picos de Europa National Park. The region is characterized by unique karst formations, including natural blowholes and inland beaches, alongside lush forests and river valleys. Trails vary from coastal paths to more challenging mountain ascents in the Sierra del Cuera, providing a range of experiences for hikers. Best hiking trails around Llanes The most popular hiking route is Llanes and Celorio Coastal Trail, a 9.2 miles (14.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 52 minutes to complete. This moderate path combines clifftop views with access to secluded beaches and meadows. Another top favourite among local hikers is Bufones de Pría Route, a moderate 9.3 miles (14.9 km) path. This route features coastal scenery and the natural blowholes of Bufones de Pría. Local hikers also love the The Enchanted Trail, a 6.2 miles (10.0 km) trail leading through Asturian forests with mythical wooden sculptures, often completed in about 2 hours 52 minutes. Hiking around Llanes is defined by dramatic coastal paths, karst formations, and inland forest routes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging mountain trails. The routes in Llanes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 1100 reviews. More than 7000 hikers have used komoot to explore Llanes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of hiking trails can I expect around Llanes?
A: Llanes offers a diverse range of hiking experiences. You can explore rugged coastal paths with dramatic cliffs, hidden caves, and beautiful bays, or venture inland to lush forests and river valleys. The region is known for unique karst formations, including natural blowholes like the Bufones de Pría, and even semi-hidden inland beaches. Trails also extend towards the foothills of the Picos de Europa National Park and the Sierra del Cuera mountain range.

Q: How many hiking trails are available in the Llanes area?
A: There are over 250 hiking routes available around Llanes, catering to various preferences and skill levels. This includes a mix of easy walks, moderate trails, and more challenging mountain ascents.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see on hikes in Llanes?
A: Hiking in Llanes allows you to discover spectacular natural features. Along the coast, you can witness the famous &lt;strong&gt;Bufones de Pría&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;Bufones de Arenillas&lt;/strong&gt;, which are natural blowholes. The region also boasts over 30 pristine beaches, some with unique karst rock formations. Inland, you can explore 'fairy-tale forests' and picturesque valleys, with views extending to the majestic Picos de Europa National Park and the Sierra del Cuera mountain range. You might also encounter interesting geological formations like those found in the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5583861" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cuevona of Cuevas del Agua&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5651091" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva del Dragón&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking trails in Llanes?
A: Yes, Llanes offers several options suitable for families. Easy walks like the Bufones de Pría Trail (2.2 miles) are great for a relaxed outing. The coastal route from Llanes to Celorio, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1314633255/llanes-and-celorio-coastal-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Llanes and Celorio Coastal Trail&lt;/a&gt;, is also ideal for families, combining clifftop views with access to secluded beaches and meadows.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Llanes?
A: Llanes offers beautiful hiking opportunities throughout much of the year. The diverse landscapes, from coastal paths to mountain foothills, mean you can find suitable trails in different seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures for hiking, while summer is ideal for combining hikes with visits to the region's many beaches. Always check local weather conditions before heading out.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Llanes?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1314565469/the-enchanted-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Enchanted Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 6.2-mile (10.0 km) loop through Asturian forests featuring mythical wooden sculptures. Another circular route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1314620419/el-camino-de-espines-from-playa-de-la-canal-circular-via-playa-de-cuevas-and-de-guadamia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Camino de Espines from Playa de La Canal&lt;/a&gt;, offering coastal scenery.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Llanes?
A: The hiking routes in Llanes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 1100 reviews. Hikers often praise the dramatic coastal paths, the unique karst formations, and the charming inland forest routes. The variety of trails, suitable for different ability levels, is also a frequently highlighted positive aspect.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: For more experienced hikers, Llanes offers challenging routes, particularly in the mountain areas. The ascent to Pico Cabeza Ubena in the Sierra del Cuera, for example, is an 11 km hike that requires good fitness and rewards with unparalleled 360-degree panoramic views encompassing both the sea and the Picos de Europa.

Q: Can I find trails that combine coastal views with forest sections?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in Llanes offer a blend of coastal and inland scenery. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1314633255/llanes-and-celorio-coastal-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Llanes and Celorio Coastal Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, combining clifftop views with access to secluded beaches and meadows. The broader Llanes Coastal Path also features sections where lush forests meet the sea.

Q: Are there any trails that are part of the Camino de Santiago?
A: Yes, a section of the &lt;strong&gt;Camino del Norte&lt;/strong&gt;, one of the historic pilgrimage routes to Santiago de Compostela, passes through Llanes. This provides options for longer, historically significant walks for those interested in combining hiking with cultural exploration.

Q: What kind of wildlife or natural environments can I expect to see?
A: Llanes is rich in natural environments. Along the coast, you'll find rugged cliffs and diverse marine ecosystems. Inland, the 'fairy-tale forests' and valleys like the Dark Valley (Valle Oscuru) are home to traditional farmhouses, watermills, and fern-covered meadows, offering a glimpse into the region's rural charm and biodiversity.

If Playa de Cuevas is very crowded, Playa de San Antonio is a beautiful alternative. Following the path, it is reached in fifteen minutes from the Parking on the road. Caution as it does not have a surveillance and rescue service.

Cuevas del Mar Beach is one of the best-known beaches in Asturias. It's located in a bay protected by rock walls. The sea has carved out crevices, grottos, and caves in the rock, hence its name. Along the access path, you can enjoy a meal and a refreshing drink at a pleasant beach bar.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Bufones de Pría Route?
faqs.answer: Parking is typically available outside the village of Llames de Pría. From there, it's a short walk to the start of the trail.

faqs.question: Is the Bufones de Pría Route suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is generally considered easy to low difficulty, making it accessible for a wide range of hikers, including families. It's a moderate hike according to komoot, with a height gain of around 196 meters.

faqs.question: Is the Bufones de Pría Route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the research doesn't explicitly state dog rules, coastal trails in Asturias are often dog-friendly. However, it's always best to keep dogs on a leash, especially near cliffs and in protected areas, and to check local signage for any specific restrictions.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the Bufones de Pría Route?
faqs.answer: The route can be enjoyed year-round. However, to witness the spectacular 'bufones' in full action, it's best to visit during high tide and when the sea is rough, which can happen in any season but is perhaps more common outside of peak summer.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Bufones de Pría Route, or specific regulations within the Protected Landscape of the Eastern Coast of Asturias?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are mentioned for hiking the Bufones de Pría Route. As it lies within the Protected Landscape of the Eastern Coast of Asturias, general regulations for protected natural areas apply, such as staying on marked paths, not disturbing wildlife, and leaving no trace.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers stunning coastal views and unique geological formations. Key highlights include the famous Pría Blowholes, Guadamía Beach, La Canal Beach, and the beautiful Cuevas del Mar Beach. You'll also pass by Fuente de las Grallas and Pozo de los Picones.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the route, and what footwear is recommended?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, featuring asphalted paths from Llames de Pría, transitioning to unpaved paths and trails along the clifftops. You might encounter natural rock formations and even walk across a natural rock bridge. Comfortable clothing and appropriate footwear, preferably sneakers or light hiking shoes, are recommended.

faqs.question: When is the best time to see the Bufones de Pría in action?
faqs.answer: To experience the full spectacle of the 'bufones' – where seawater and air are forced through cracks, creating natural geysers – you should aim to visit during high tide and when the sea is rough. This creates the most dramatic display, with water jets reaching up to 20 meters high.

faqs.question: Is the Bufones de Pría Route a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Komoot route for the Bufones de Pría is a circular hike, starting and ending near the same point. This makes it convenient for parking and completing the full experience.

faqs.question: Does the Bufones de Pría Route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Ruta de los Bufones de Pría and the European long distance path E9 east Asturies.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restrooms near the trail?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is primarily natural, the starting point in Llames de Pría village and nearby areas will likely have local cafes, restaurants, and potentially restrooms. It's advisable to check for specific opening times, especially outside of peak season.

faqs.question: Can I hike the Bufones de Pría Route in winter?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route can be hiked in winter. In fact, winter often brings rougher seas, which can enhance the spectacle of the bufones. Just be prepared for potentially colder, windier, and wetter conditions, and ensure you have appropriate gear.
